learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an in-depth knowledge about the dynamic nature of business and the role of strategy in generating and sustaining competitive advantage.
Investigate and discuss the most important internal and external influences on an organisations strategy.
Evaluate business strategies, discussing how particular courses of action may (or may not) provide competitive advantage.
Reflect critically on the issues involved in integrating the various functional activities of a firm into a coherent strategy.
Show an awareness of the fundamental uncertainties that strategic managers face in charting a firms course over time, articulating consequences and proposing solutions to resolve these challenges.
Participate collaboratively and productively as a member of a team.

assessments.due_date:
During Your Seminar Week 6,

To be completed and handed in during your regular seminar.

The task must be completed in-class. Identity will be verified, so please ensure you have your student card with you.

No electronic materials will be permitted for completing this assignment, so please ensure you bring writing materials.

You may bring in one A4 (double sided) set of handwritten notes to assist you in the assessment item. Handwritten notes to be completed in English only. Materials will be removed if writing is produced using a different language.
assessments.task_description:
You will be provided an AI-generated PESTEL, Porter's Five Forces, and VRIN analysis of a case organisation. Your task is to use the learning you have acquired on PESTEL, Porter, and VRIN frameworks in weeks 2-4 to improve on the AI generated analysis.

The assessment is to be completed individually. It is to be completed using written materials only. No electronic materials are permitted. The assessment item must be handed in prior to leaving class.

AI Statement:

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: The assessment item cannot be handed in late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
Business Simulation 1 Week 8,

Business Simulation 2 Week 9,

Business Simulation 3 Week 11,

Business Simulation 4 Week 12,

Work must be handed in in-class during weeks 8, 9, 11, and 12.
assessments.task_description:
Students will be allocated to teams and each member will be assigned to play a role on the management team of the case company. During the seminar, the management team will be asked to evaluate and make recommendations on the case company's business-level, corporate-level, collaborative strategies, and corporate governance. Students will be assessed on the quality of the strategic advice that their team provides for the case company, as well as their ability to understand and leverage organisational politics in arguing for their preferred strategy.

You will have four opportunities to be assessed on your performance in the Business Simulation, and your group's best three (out of four) marks will count towards your final grade for the course (3 x 13.33 = 40%).

Students who are absent for a Business Simulation meeting will receive a score of zero for that week. A student who has legitimate grounds for an absence from a Business Simulation meeting under the University's assessment policy must contact the Course Coordinator prior to, or within 24 hours of, the missed assessment and must provide appropriate supporting documentation.

Your student ID will be checked for each of the four weeks of this assessment item to verify your identity. Please ensure you bring your ID to class.

The assessment task is to completed using handwritten materials. Use of electronic devices is not permitted. Students found using electronic devices will be asked to leave the classroom and will be scored at a zero for that week's assessment.

To assist with their work, groups will be provided with printed course materials related to political games, elites, and an AI-generated PESTEL, Porter, and VRIN analysis of their Wesfarmers business unit.

At the conclusion of their work, groups are to inform the teaching team. One student from the group will then be permitted to take a photo of their team's output for upload into the course Blackboard site and through the relevant assessment submission link for that week. Accessing sites other than email and the course Blackboard site is not permitted. Groups should allow at least ten minutes for completion of the upload process.

AI Statement

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission will not be accepted and will result in a mark of zero for that week.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Word Limit: 1200 words (+ or - 10%)

In this individual assignment, you will reflect on your personal experience of the Business Simulation to better understand what you did well, what you could improve, and what you would do differently next time.

To enable this reflection, your assignment will do the following:

  • Describe two instances from the Business Simulation in weeks 8, 9, 11, and 12 of challenges your Business Unit or Group faced in negotiating strategic recommendations with other Business Units and Groups in the corporation.
  • Leverage theory on strategic management and organisational politics to justify where you believe your Business Unit or Group was successful in pursuing implementation of your strategic recommendations.
  • Leverage theory on strategic management and organisational politics to justify where you believe your Business Unit or Group was unsuccessful or could have performed better in pursuing implementation of your strategic recommendations.
  • Leverage theory on organisational politics to explain how you would engage differently in networking, social astuteness, and persuasion, were you to pursue negotiation around implementation of your strategic recommendations for a second time. Even if your negotiations were successful, it should be that you can identify political strategies which would have furthered your Business Unit’s strategic ambitions.
  • Engage in effective communication of key messages around strategy and political theory.

Please note that a successful negotiation may not mean that your Business Unit or Group had its initial strategic recommendation accepted in whole. Other Business Units or Groups can improve a strategic recommendation. It is not a winner-take-all situation. A successful outcome for your Business Unit or Group should be one in which you recognise a justifiable strategy has been adopted for assisting the corporation and its subsidiaries in pursuit of their overall vision.

AI Statement:

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Course Introduction: What is strategy?

In this course introduction, you will be presented with the assessment activities and an explanation of how the course will work as well as some understanding on how theorists have viewed the topic of strategic management over the years.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Course Introduction: What is strategy? In this course introduction, you will be presented with the assessment activities and an explanation of how the course will work as well as some understanding on how theorists have viewed the topic of strategic management over the years.

learning_activities.topic:
External Environment Analysis: PESTEL

This week you will learn about PESTEL analysis. See how it was derived from the thoughts of Aguilar (1967) and learn how to apply the framework in evaluating the general external environment of the organisation. Importantly, you will be introduced to key course criteria for critiquing the quality of an AI-produced PESTEL analysis. You will also have opportunity to practice applying these criteria to an AI-generated piece of work and receive feedback from teaching staff on your efforts. This seminar is essential to your preparation for the test in week 5.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: External Environment Analysis: PESTEL This week you will learn about PESTEL analysis. See how it was derived from the thoughts of Aguilar (1967) and learn how to apply the framework in evaluating the general external environment of the organisation. Importantly, you will be introduced to key course criteria for critiquing the quality of an AI-produced PESTEL analysis. You will also have opportunity to practice applying these criteria to an AI-generated piece of work and receive feedback from teaching staff on your efforts. This seminar is essential to your preparation for the test in week 5.

learning_activities.topic:
External Environment Analysis: Porter's Five Forces

This week you will learn about Porter's Five Forces industry analysis. See how it was derived from the thoughts of Porter (1979) and learn how to apply the framework in evaluating the external industry environment of the organisation. Importantly, you will be introduced to key course criteria for critiquing the quality of an AI-produced Porter's Five Forces analysis. You will also have opportunity to practice applying these criteria to an AI-generated piece of work and receive feedback from teaching staff on your efforts. This seminar is essential to your preparation for the test in week 5.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: External Environment Analysis: Porter's Five Forces This week you will learn about Porter's Five Forces industry analysis. See how it was derived from the thoughts of Porter (1979) and learn how to apply the framework in evaluating the external industry environment of the organisation. Importantly, you will be introduced to key course criteria for critiquing the quality of an AI-produced Porter's Five Forces analysis. You will also have opportunity to practice applying these criteria to an AI-generated piece of work and receive feedback from teaching staff on your efforts. This seminar is essential to your preparation for the test in week 5.

learning_activities.topic:
Internal Environment Analysis: VRIN

This week you will learn about VRIN analysis. See how it was derived from the thoughts of Barney (1991) and learn how to apply the framework in evaluating the internal environment of the organisation. Importantly, you will be introduced to key course criteria for critiquing the quality of an AI-produced VRIN analysis. You will also have opportunity to practice applying these criteria to an AI-generated piece of work and receive feedback from teaching staff on your efforts. This seminar is essential to your preparation for the test in week 5.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Internal Environment Analysis: VRIN This week you will learn about VRIN analysis. You will also have opportunity to practice applying these criteria to an AI-generated piece of work and receive feedback from teaching staff on your efforts. learning_activities.topic:
In-class Practice In Readiness For Assignment 1

This week's seminar will be essential preparation for the test associated with your first assignment.

You will be placed into groups with other students. You will then be presented with an AI generated PESTEL, Porter, and VRIN analysis of a case study organisation. Your role in your groups is to apply the criteria associated with PESTEL, Porter, and VRIN analysis as outlined in weeks 2-4 and to assess the quality of the AI generated output you have been provided. You will receive feedback from the teaching team on the quality of your work. The intention is that you will use this week's work to practice applying the criteria for assessing the AI produced output, thereby becoming better prepared for the test to be conducted in week 6.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L06

learning_activities.topic:
Case Study Analysis: Individual In-class assessment

This assessment is to be completed as an individual task.

This week you will engage in your in-class assessment. You will be provided with an AI generated PESTEL, Porter's Five Forces, and VRIN analysis of a case study organisation. Your task will be to apply your understanding of key criteria introduced in weeks 2, 3, and 4 to assess the quality of the AI generated output.

You must submit your assessment prior to leaving class. Please note that no electronic materials will be permitted for use in this test. However, you may bring along one A4 sheet (double sided and written only in English language) compiled with handwritten notes to assist you. Further information on the test can be found on the course BlackBoard site.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Case Study Analysis: Individual In-class assessment This assessment is to be completed as an individual task. learning_activities.topic:
In-Semester Break

Use this week to finalise your first assignment
learning_activities.topic_description: In-Semester Break Use this week to finalise your first assignment

learning_activities.topic:
The Politics of Strategy

This week will introduce you to the idea of strategy as politics. How do successful strategists navigate political games and elites in order to implement strategy? The session will also serve as a practice for our in-class assessment in the coming weeks. You will come together in your teams and practice your decision making and negotiation skills.

Please note this seminar is essential preparation for the second assignment.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: The Politics of Strategy This week will introduce you to the idea of strategy as politics. How do successful strategists navigate political games and elites in order to implement strategy? The session will also serve as a practice for our in-class assessment in the coming weeks. You will come together in your teams and practice your decision making and negotiation skills. Please note this seminar is essential preparation for the second assignment.

learning_activities.topic:
Business-Level Strategy

This week will be the first of the in-class assessments for your assignment on the Business Simulation. You will explore the topic of business-level strategy by making decisions and negotiating on behalf of your Wesfarmers Strategic Business Unit.

Please note no electronic materials are permitted for completing this exercise. More information on the assessment item can be found on the course BlackBoard site.
